name: Mindset: The New Psychology of Success

children.name: The Two Mindsets
Fixed Mindset
Belief that qualities are carved in stone
Urgency to prove intelligence and character
Avoidance of challenges to protect ego
Effort seen as sign of low ability
Failure defined as a permanent identity
Growth Mindset
Belief that qualities can be cultivated
Passion for learning and stretching oneself
Thriving on challenges and setbacks
Effort seen as the path to mastery
Failure defined as information for growth
Impact in Different Domains
Sports
The 'Natural' vs. the hard worker
Character: digging deep under pressure
Case studies: Michael Jordan and Tiger Woods
Business
CEO Disease: worshiping talent over process
Fixed mindset failures: Enron and Lee Iacocca
Growth mindset leaders: Jack Welch and Lou Gerstner
Organizational cultures of development
Education
Teachers' mindsets affecting student potential
High standards combined with nurturing
The danger of praising intelligence vs. process
Relationships
Rejection as a judgment vs. learning experience
Belief that relationships require work
Fostering the partner's development
Origin and Change
Messages from Authority Figures
Praising process, effort, and strategy
Constructive criticism as a teaching tool
Avoidance of labels and fixed judgments
The Journey to Change
Mindsets as powerful but changeable beliefs
Awareness of the fixed mindset voice
Taking the growth-minded path to new challenges
